steps.title: Determine the condition for no real solution
steps.content:
The left side $-(3x-7)^2$ is the negative of a perfect square, so it is always non-positive.

Its maximum value is 0 (at $x = \frac{7}{3}$), meaning the left side can achieve any value in the interval $(-\infty, 0]$.

For the equation to have a real solution, the right side $p + 17$ must lie in this achievable range.

For no real solution, the right side must be outside the range: $p + 17 > 0$, which gives $p > -17$.

steps.title: Find the least integer value of p
steps.content: We need the smallest integer satisfying $p > -17$. That integer is $p = -16$. This matches choice C — C is correct.
